description.value:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Stability: 0

an4b9HdNrni1iSlM.name: Purchased Traits
an4b9HdNrni1iSlM.description: You have 3 ancestry points to spend on the following traits.

description.value: As a maneuver, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 squares, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.
Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.
When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

Nomad lines that winter beside rib-cairns and bone-fields, keeping the old fires and the old taboos while carrying authority by rite and remembrance.

Language: Beinmarksmål

Environment: Nomadic; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Noble

Skills: Perception, Survival, Handle Animals.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 100

Perk: *Rapid Relocation*. Once per respite, you can reduce the travel time of a short leg of a journey for your group, provided conditions allow mounted or guided movement.
When you make a test using a skill from the exploration skill group and at least one of the d10s rolled is a 1, you can reroll one d10. You can use this perk only once per test.

The whole world’s a stage, and everyone on it, an actor. No one knows this better than the troubadour. You find energy in the drama of everyday life and know how to draw spectacle forth from even the most mundane of situations. You accent highs and deepen lows in service to whoever might witness your performance. As a troubadour, you chase drama. The insurmountable dangers of the world might cause many a hero to cower. But you take to that world stage not intending to die, but to find out if you are truly alive

XsWPIcFxdWOgB9mr.description: Choose two skills from the interpersonal skill group and one skill from the intrigue or lore skill groups. (Quick Build: Brag, Flirt, Read Person, Rumors.)

RnlXI12YDhR68bUI.description: Choose a troubadour class act from the following options, each of which grants you a skill. (Quick Build: Virtuoso.)

biz5s6KQHKhXIddz.name: Signature Ability
biz5s6KQHKhXIddz.description: Choose one signature ability from the following options. Signature abilities can be used at will. (Quick Build: Witty Banter.)

FTRNYKzPAPe8ihAj.name: 3 Drama Ability
FTRNYKzPAPe8ihAj.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 3 drama to use. (Quick Build: Harsh Critic.)

Wq0LVQSvUo2IUflL.name: 5 Drama Ability
Wq0LVQSvUo2IUflL.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 5 drama to use. (Quick Build: Dramatic Reversal.)

Vtjk6oGiz0SZo3Sx.name: Kit
Vtjk6oGiz0SZo3Sx.description: You can use and gain the benefits of a kit. (Quick Build: Swashbuckler.)

bUc7AbUQrg38DeXx.description: You have a specific manner that helps define your presence on the battlefield. Choose one of the following features.

6XbB9blJmaxpLji3.description: Your Agility and Presence scores each increase to 3.

1X8QXHMoKGEJVgjy.description: Each of your characteristic scores increases by 1, to a maximum of 4.

O4LLYyPMwqF0FH3e.description: Your Agility and Presence scores each increase to 5.

4QttNEOgEc0uPQQX.name: Zeitgeist
4QttNEOgEc0uPQQX.description: You always have your ear to the ground, your finger on the pulse. When you start or finish a respite, choose one of the following effects.

aAAliCcceruLJIWC.description: You have the following performance ability, which is usable with your Routines feature.

CpJAaH7F6PZ5YzIm.name: 11 Drama Ability
CpJAaH7F6PZ5YzIm.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 11 drama to use.

7M927rywbCYCCBtK.name: Roar of the Crowd
7M927rywbCYCCBtK.description: You are empowered by your audience, near and far.

2AdZJCuCbbuyI9m7.name: Melodrama
2AdZJCuCbbuyI9m7.description: You have more ways of getting the most drama out of a situation.

description.value:
You can give a rousing speech, invoke your inspirations, or lift your fellows’ spirits, appealing to the muses to heighten a battle’s drama. However, irony is eager to hand your fortune to the villain to achieve the same end.

Before you roll to gain drama at the start of your turn, you can make your appeal (no action required). If you do, your roll gains the following additional effects:

  • If the roll is a 1, you [[/gain 1 heroic]]{ gain 1 additional drama}. The Director gains [[/roll 1d3]] Malice.

  • If the roll is a 2, you [[/gain 1 heroic]], which you can keep or give to an ally within the distance of your active performance. The Director gains 1 Malice.

  • If the roll is a 3, you [[/gain 2 heroic]], which you can distribute among yourself and any allies within the distance of your active performance.

During battles, you are fueled by the dynamic ups, downs, and upside downs of the fray, from which you derive a Heroic Resource called drama.

Drama in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain drama equal to your Victories. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 1d3 drama. Additionally, you gain drama when certain events occur during a combat encounter:

  • The first time three or more heroes use an ability on the same turn, you gain 2 drama.

  • The first time any hero is made winded during the encounter, you gain 2 drama.

  • Whenever a creature within your line of effect rolls a natural 19 or 20, you gain 3 drama.

  • When you or another hero dies, you gain 10 drama.

When you are dead, you continue to gain drama during combat as long as your body is intact. If you have 30 drama during the encounter in which you died, you can come back to life with 1 Stamina and 0 drama (no action required). If you are still dead after the encounter in which you died, you can’t gain drama during future encounters.

You lose any remaining drama at the end of the encounter.

Drama Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain drama outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost drama without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs drama, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Victories or finish a respite.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited drama on its effect, such as Artful Flourish, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of drama equal to your Victories.

You enter every battle with a set of performance abilities at the ready. Performances are magical presentations (such as songs, dances, poems, or gymnastic feats) that your allies can participate in. These abilities have the Performance keyword. At the start of each combat round, as long as you are not dazed, dead, or surprised, you can either choose a new performance or maintain your current performance (no action required). Your performance lasts until you are unable to maintain it or until the end of the encounter.

You start off with the Choreography and Revitalizing Limerick performance abilities. Your choice of class act grants you additional performances.

effect.before: While this performance is active, each target who starts their turn in the area gains a [[/apply KfIAesBF6E0W0mX4]]{+2 bonus to speed} until the end of their turn.

system.story: There once was a man from Capital...

effect.before: At the end of each of your turns while this performance is active, you can choose up to a number of targets equal to [[lookup @P]]{your Presence score}. Each chosen target can spend a Recovery.

description.value:
Whenever you obtain a success on a test to interact with an NPC using a skill from the interpersonal group, you can form a bond with that NPC. When you enter into a negotiation with a bonded NPC, their patience increases by 1 (to a maximum of 5). Additionally, the first time during a negotiation that you personally make an argument that would increase a bonded NPC’s interest by 1, you instead increase their interest by 2 (to a maximum of 5).

You can have a number of bonds active equal to your level. When you form a bond with a new NPC that would exceed the limit, you must choose which of your active bonds to lose.

items.name: Allow Me to Introduce Tonight’s Players

description.value:
Whenever you take the first turn in a combat encounter, you can use a main action to introduce yourself and your allies to your opponents.

Each ally can shift up to their speed, and ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the end of the combat round. Additionally,any surprised enemy is no longer surprised.
The Whirlwind kit makes effective use of whips, granting you mobility, damage, and reach. If you want to be a fast-moving warrior who lashes foes with a chain or whip, then this is the kit for you

system.story: When you draw your whip back after an attack, your enemy is drawn ever closer

description.value: You are a leading expert on a particular subject. Choose one skill you have from the lore skill group. You always have a double edge on tests made to recall lore using this skill. Additionally, your specialist knowledge grants you notoriety in fields related to the chosen skill. You treat your Renown as 1 higher when negotiating with an NPC who knows your reputation, or 2 higher if they have the same skill you chose for this perk.

system.story: A lyrical (and physical) jab insults an enemy and inspires an ally.

effect.after: One ally within 10 squares of you can end one effect on them that is ended by a saving throw or that ends at the end of their turn.

spend.text: The chosen ally can spend a Recovery.

description.value: You seek drama from story and recount, using your magic to manipulate the sequence of events unfolding before you. You have the Brag skill.

nN3C2pYVlVRjscYI.description: You have the following performance ability, which is usable with your Routines feature.

ppFVqFr87jeE0E9L.description: Your troubadour class act grants your choice of one of two heroic abilities.

system.story: No, no, no, you lose the audience that way. Try it like this …

effect.before: At the end of each of your turns while this performance is active, you can choose up to a number of targets equal to [[lookup @P]]{your Presence score} and teleport those targets to unoccupied spaces in the area. A target can’t be teleported in a way that would harm them (such as over a cliff), leave them dying, or result in them suffering a condition or other negative effect.

system.story: It doesn’t need to make sense. Just say it with emotion.

effect.before:
Choose one of the following effects:

  • You orate a rousing tale of victory. One ally within distance gains an edge on the next power roll they make before the start of your next turn.

  • You weave a tale of high-stakes heroics. One ally within distance [[/surge 1]].

  • You insult a foe where they’re most vulnerable. One enemy with in distance takes a bane on the next power roll they make before the end of their next turn.

spend.text: You can choose two targets for the chosen effect.

system.story: All’s fair in love and whatever.
system.trigger: The target makes an ability roll that has an edge, a double  edge, a bane, or a double bane.

effect.before: An edge on the triggering roll becomes a bane, or a double edge becomes an edge. A bane becomes an edge, or a double bane becomes a bane.

spend.text: An edge on the triggering roll becomes a double  bane, or a double edge is negated. A bane becomes a double edge, or  a double bane is negated.

system.story: We offered them a percentage of the gross. So they’re working for free!

effect.before:
A guest star appears to help you during the encounter: either a bystander within distance uplifted by your magic, or a mysterious new hero who appears in an unoccupied space within distance.

This guest star is controlled by you, has their own turn, and shares your characteristics. Their Stamina maximum is half yours. They have no abilities other than your melee and ranged free strikes.

At the end of the encounter, or when the guest star is reduced to 0 Stamina, they retreat or revert to a bystander. The same bystander can’t be uplifted this way more than once during an encounter.

system.story: You produce an entrancing note that twists the senses in a spectacu lar fashion.

spend.text: The size of the burst increases by 1 for every 2  drama spent.

system.story: O happy dagger, this is thy sheath!

effect.before: You turn [[/apply invisible]] and create a magical illusion of your corpse falling in your space. While you are invisible, you gain a +3 bonus to speed and you ignore difficult terrain. The illusion and your invisibility last until the end of your next turn, or until the illusion is interacted with, you take damage, or you use a main action or a maneuver.
Choose an enemy adjacent to you. The next ability power roll an ally who makes against that creature before the start of your next turn has an edge.

A creature who uses the Catch Breath maneuver spends a Recovery and regains [[/heal @recoveries.recoveryValue]]{Stamina} equal to their recovery value.

A creature who is dying can’t use the Catch Breath maneuver, but other creatures can help them spend Recoveries in other ways.

When a creature takes the Charge main action, they move up to their speed in a straight line without jumping, then make a @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.wU69Y06G9lYFrvp6]{melee free strike} against a target when they end their move. If the creature has an ability with the Charge keyword, they can use that ability against the target instead of a free strike.

A creature can’t move through difficult terrain or shift when they charge. They can fly or burrow as part of the Charge main action if they have that movement available to them, but they can’t climb or swim while charging unless they can automatically use that movement at full speed.

effects.name: Defending
effects.description: When a creature takes the Defend main action, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creature’s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.

effect.before: When a creature takes the [[/apply TYggdI2Kwa302NjW]]{Defend main action}, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creature’s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.
A creature who is grabbed by another creature, an object, or an effect (see Grab below) can attempt to escape by using the following ability.
effect.after: You take a bane on this maneuver if your size is smaller than the size of the creature, object, or effect that has you grabbed.

tier2.display: You can grab the target, but if you do, the target can make a melee free strike against you before they are grabbed

tier3.display: The target is grabbed by you.

effect.before: A creature seeking to keep a foe close and locked down can attempt to grab a creature using the following ability
effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature who uses the Heal main action employs medicine or inspiring words to make an adjacent creature feel better and stay in the fight. The target creature can spend a Recovery to regain Stamina, or can make a saving throw against one effect they are suffering that is ended by a saving throw.

effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature can use the Stand Up maneuver to stand up if they are prone, ending that condition. Alternatively, they can use this maneuver to make a willing adjacent prone creature stand up.
When a creature takes the Advance move action, they move a number of squares up to their speed. They can break up this movement with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

When a creature takes the Disengage move action, they can shift [[lookup @movement.disengage]]{1} square(s). Certain class features, kits, and other rules allow a creature to shift more than 1 square when they disengage. A creature who does so can break up their shift with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

A creature can take the Ride move action only while mounted on another creature. When a creature takes the Ride move action, they cause their mount to move up to the mount’s speed, taking the rider with them. Alternatively, a creature can use the Ride move action to have their mount use the Disengage move action as a free triggered action. A creature can use the Ride move action only once per round. A mounted creature can only have this move action applied to them once per round. This movement can be broken up with the rider’s maneuver and main action however they wish.

biography.value:
Söldís Glóðfari is the master of the Glóðfari caravan — a well-worn group of six wagons that have been making circuits through the failing settlements of Svellheim for many seasons. Her coat is patchwork repairs, each patch from a different place. Around her neck, on a cord close to her chest, hangs a sealed clay pot. Something glows inside — not quite flame, but the suggestion of something that has been burning for a very, very long time.

She reads people the way other people read maps. She notices things before she remarks on them. She is generous with what she can give, and precise about what she cannot.

She will be present during the Siege of Hrafnborg. Her caravan and its people will be there. The party is already deciding, without knowing it, whether those people survive.
biography.director:
First encounter: Beat 4b — in a sheltered hollow just past the Greypass toll. Her caravan has been through the same checkpoint but via a longer, quieter standoff. She sees the party coming before they see her and has already formed opinions.

The sealed clay pot: Do not reveal this yet. If someone asks, Söldís says 'That's for later' and changes the subject without hesitation. She has done this thousands of times. The pot contains a preserved ember from a settlement that no longer exists — one of the first to go dark. She has been carrying it for years, waiting for someone who can use it.

Kaelen's ember-mark: She notices it. She does not comment immediately. A small change in expression — not fear, recognition. If Kaelen catches this and presses, she says: 'You carry something. I just noticed the shadow it casts.' She will not elaborate in this scene.

Caravan members and their benefits:

  • Ragna (70s, herb-woman): dried herbs grant +1 edge on one Recovery test during next rest
  • Vélaug (35, repair-master): silently restores one piece of damaged gear to full function
  • Söldís's information: +1 edge on one knowledge or navigation test in next beat
  • The warm meal: counts as a Recovery for one party member

Recurring arc: Söldís appears in Acts 1-3. Each time the party encounters her, she knows a little more. By Act 3, she will be at Hrafnborg's gates with her caravan and its people during the siege. The warmth she shows the party here is the warmth they will want to protect there.
